data.title: A whale withdrew 4,472 ETH from Kraken 3 hours ago and currently holds 8,855 ETH.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, address 0xe5e withdrew 4,472 ETH from Kraken 3 hours ago, worth approximately $10 million. This address currently holds a total of 8,855 ETH, worth approximately $20 million.

data.title: The probability of Polymarket's "Printr public sale raising over $4 million" has fallen to 63%, a drop of over 30% within the day.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of Printr raising over $4 million in its public sale on Polymarket has dropped to 63%, a decrease of over 30% within the day, with a trading volume of nearly $3.26 million. Printr is a cross-chain token issuance platform that allows users and AI agents to issue, trade, and stake tokens in a multi-chain environment. It is currently deployed on eight blockchains, including Solana, Base, BNB Chain, and Ethereum, and offers features such as customizable fee structures, cross-chain transactions, and staking incentive mechanisms. Data shows that the project has currently raised over $2.46 million, with approximately 35 hours remaining until the fundraising ends. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, anticipating changes before pricing.

data.title: Samsung Electronics Reports Record Quarterly Profits
data.description: Samsung Electronics reported a significant increase in its first-quarter operating profit, which rose by 756.1% year-on-year to 57.23 trillion won (approximately $38.5 billion), setting a new quarterly record. According to Jin10, the majority of this profit was driven by its chip business. Additionally, revenue increased by 69.2% to 133.87 trillion won, while net profit surged by 474.3% to 47.23 trillion won. The Device Solutions division, responsible for its semiconductor operations, achieved revenue of 81.7 trillion won and an operating profit of 53.7 trillion won. These results significantly exceeded market expectations. Financial data provider FnGuide reported that local brokerage firms had anticipated revenue of 117.13 trillion won and operating profit of 38.12 trillion won over the past month. Samsung Electronics' latest quarterly revenue and operating profit figures surpassed the previous record highs set in the prior quarter, which were 93.84 trillion won and 20.07 trillion won, respectively.

data.title: Citic Securities Maintains Forecast for Fed Rate Cut Under Warsh
data.description: On April 30, Citic Securities released a report stating that it maintains its previous view, projecting a single 25 basis point rate cut in the second half of the year if Kevin Warsh becomes the Federal Reserve Chairman. According to Jin10, the report emphasizes the importance of closely monitoring the statements of the 12 current voting members of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC). The path of the Federal Reserve's monetary policy is increasingly dependent on the balance of votes among FOMC members, while the individual speeches of the Fed Chair have less influence on market guidance compared to the past.

data.title: U.S. Government Seeks to Seize Oil Tankers Linked to Iran
data.description:
The U.S. government is reportedly seeking to seize two oil tankers intercepted by the U.S. Navy, which are linked to Iran. According to Odaily, a senior White House official disclosed that the U.S. Department of Justice has initiated the seizure process. However, the official did not provide details on the procedure or clarify whether the move involves confiscating the crude oil on board.

The official, who requested anonymity, cited 'operational security' as the reason for not disclosing the final disposition of the vessels or commenting on their current routes. According to the U.S. Department of Defense, the U.S. Navy intercepted and boarded the two tankers on April 20 and 22 in the Indian Ocean, suspecting them of transporting oil from Iran. The report noted that the tankers continued to sail in the Indian Ocean in the following days, apparently changing course multiple times.

data.title: The US XRP spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $3.5914 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, XRP spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $3.5914 million yesterday (April 29th, Eastern Time). The XRP spot ETF with the largest net inflow yesterday was the Bitwise XRP ETF (XRP), with a net inflow of $2.1204 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $428 million. The second largest was the Franklin XRP ETF (XRPZ), with a net inflow of $1.4711 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $350 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs was $1.036 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.23%, and a cumulative historical net inflow of $1.296 billion.

data.title: SoftBank plans to launch and list its AI company "Roze" in the United States.
data.description: According to the Financial Times, sources revealed that SoftBank Group plans to establish and list an independent intelligent robotics and data center company called "Roze" in the United States. SoftBank plans to list Roze this year, targeting a valuation of $100 billion. SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son is driving this process, a move aimed at offsetting the company's tens of billions of dollars invested in artificial intelligence. SoftBank has been continuously increasing its debt, and Son is trying to position himself as a key figure in the global AI boom, having made a large-scale investment in OpenAI. (Jinshi)

data.title: AI TRENDS | SoftBank Plans to Launch and List Roze in the U.S. with $100 Billion Valuation
data.description: SoftBank Group is reportedly planning to establish and list an independent company named Roze in the United States, according to the Financial Times. The company, focused on smart robotics and data centers, is expected to go public within this year with a target valuation of $100 billion. SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son is actively driving this initiative to offset the company's substantial investments in the artificial intelligence sector. SoftBank has been increasing its debt, while Son aims to position himself as a key figure in the global AI boom, having made significant investments in OpenAI.

data.title: US media: US government seeks to "seize" two oil tankers linked to Iran.
data.description: Bloomberg reported on the 29th that, according to a senior White House official, the US government is seeking to "seize" two oil tankers linked to Iran that were recently intercepted by the US Navy. The report stated that the official said the US Department of Justice has initiated "seizure" procedures, but he did not elaborate on the specifics of these procedures or whether this indicated the US planned to "seize" the crude oil carried on the tankers. The official, who requested anonymity, also declined to disclose the final fate of the vessels, citing "operational security," and refused to comment on their current course. According to a statement from the US Department of Defense, the US Navy intercepted and boarded two oil tankers "transporting oil from Iran" in the Indian Ocean on the 20th and 22nd, respectively. The report said the two tankers continued sailing in the Indian Ocean for the following days and appeared to change course several times. (Xinhua)

data.title: Fed's Potential Rate Cuts Hinge on Oil Prices and Trump, Report Says
data.description: A report from China International Capital Corporation (CICC) suggests that the U.S. Federal Reserve should ideally cut interest rates twice, according to Jin10. This optimistic outlook is based on the assumption that inflation will decrease due to a high base effect, provided oil prices do not remain above $100 until the end of the year. However, the report notes that actual implementation depends on both oil prices and U.S. President Donald Trump's actions. The ongoing tension in Iran, which keeps oil prices high, and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's cautious stance due to an investigation, are challenges that Kevin Warsh, who is set to take over in June, cannot resolve alone. The report emphasizes that a swift compromise by Trump and a resolution to the investigation into Powell could pave the way for potential rate cuts.

data.title: Japan's March Industrial Output Grows 2.3% Year-on-Year
data.description: Japan's industrial production in March increased by 2.3% compared to the same month last year, according to preliminary data. According to Jin10, this growth slightly exceeded the expected 2.2% and marked an improvement from the previous month's 0.40% increase.

data.title: An account that had lost over $3 million purchased $260,000 worth of NBA playoffs Eastern Conference First Round Game 5, where the Pistons defeated the Magic with the point spread.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, in Polymarket's prediction event for "NBA Eastern Conference First Round Game 5 Pistons vs. Magic," an account (0x492442eab586f242b53bda933fd5de859c8a3782) that lost over $3 million purchased $260,000 worth of bets on the Pistons to win by a 10.5-point spread against the Magic. The average opening price was 51.5¢, and the current price is 69.5¢, resulting in a floating loss of approximately $88,000. The Pistons lead the NBA Eastern Conference First Round series 3-1 against the Magic. Game 5 started at 7:00 AM Beijing time today, and the second quarter is currently underway. The Pistons' starting lineup is O'Sullivan, Harris, Duren, Robinson, and Cunningham; the Magic's starting lineup is Kane, Bancroft, Carter, Bane, and Sagan. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before prices are priced in.

data.title: Japan's March Retail Sales Exceed Expectations
data.description: Japan's retail sales in March increased by 1.7% year-on-year, surpassing the expected 0.8%, according to Jin10. The previous figure was revised from -0.20% to -0.10%.

data.title: AI-Driven Rally in Asia Masks Market Strain Amid US-Iran Conflict
data.description: Asia's stock markets are experiencing a rally driven by artificial intelligence advancements, Bloomberg posted on X. However, this surge in technology stocks is overshadowing the broader market's struggles, particularly due to the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran. The geopolitical tensions are impacting investor sentiment, causing concerns about potential disruptions in global trade and economic stability. While tech companies continue to benefit from AI innovations, the overall market remains under pressure as investors weigh the implications of the conflict on future growth prospects.

data.title: Shinhan Card and Solana Foundation Collaborate on Stablecoin Payment Technology
data.description: Shinhan Card has entered into a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with the Solana Foundation to develop stablecoin payment technology and enhance Web3 payment solutions. According to NS3.AI, the collaboration aims to advance a proof of concept this year, utilizing the Solana testnet to explore various customer and merchant payment scenarios.

data.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $75,000 Amid Federal Reserve's Hawkish Tone
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell announced his intention to remain on the board after his term ends in May, following the Fed's decision to maintain interest rates between 3.5% and 3.75%. According to NS3.AI, Powell cited legal and political pressures as reasons for his decision to stay rather than leave entirely. The announcement came as three Federal Reserve governors dissented, advocating for the removal of easing guidance. In response to the Fed's hawkish stance, Bitcoin's price dropped below $75,000.

data.title: Judge Denies Sam Bankman-Fried's Request for New Trial
data.description: A federal judge has denied Sam Bankman-Fried's request for a new trial following his 25-year prison sentence. According to NS3.AI, Judge Lewis Kaplan ruled that the three witnesses cited by Bankman-Fried were not newly discovered, as he was aware of them before the trial. Kaplan dismissed the motion with prejudice, preventing any similar future requests. Bankman-Fried's pending appeal remains his only option to contest the conviction, with a panel decision potentially forthcoming.

data.title: Tether Supports Merger Proposal to Boost Bitcoin Adoption
data.description: Tether has announced its intention to vote in favor of a proposed merger between Twenty One Capital and Strike through its investment arm, Tether Investments. According to NS3.AI, the proposal also includes a subsequent merger with Electron Energy. Tether believes these transactions could enhance profitability and expedite the adoption of Bitcoin. Should the deals be successful, Tether plans to recommend Electron founder and CEO Raphael Zagury for the position of president of the newly formed company.

data.title: Oil Prices Surge Above $110 Amid U.S. Blockade on Iranian Ports
data.description: Oil prices have climbed above $110 per barrel as the United States maintains its blockade on Iranian ports. According to NS3.AI, U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the blockade will remain in effect until a satisfactory agreement is reached. In response, a military advisor to Iran's supreme leader has warned of potential retaliation if the blockade persists. Dennis Kissler of BOK Financial noted that an extended blockade could drive oil prices higher, serving as a short-term bullish factor for crude.

data.title: Visa's Stablecoin Settlement Pilot Achieves $7 Billion Annualized Run Rate
data.description: Visa's stablecoin settlement pilot has reached a $7 billion annualized run rate following the expansion of support from four to nine blockchains. According to NS3.AI, Visa reported a 50% quarter-over-quarter growth in the run rate after incorporating Arc, Base, Canton, Polygon, and Tempo. Additionally, Visa announced that USDC settlement now includes U.S. banks, and the company supports over 130 stablecoin-linked card programs across more than 50 countries.
